text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Holloway, Gerald T.
MRN: [REDACTED]
DOB: [REDACTED] | SEX: Male
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-[REDACTED]
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [REDACTED], MD (Gastroenterology)
CLINICAL SERVICE: Gastroenterology / GI Endoscopy
CLINICAL HISTORY: 58-year-old male with new-onset change in bowel habits, rectal bleeding, and a 12-pound unintentional weight loss over 3 months. Colonoscopy performed for further evaluation. Sigmoid colon mass identified endoscopically; biopsies submitted for histologic assessment.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SPECIMEN SUBMITTED:
  A: Sigmoid colon mass -- forceps biopsies (x6 fragments)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

A. SIGMOID COLON, MASS, COLONOSCOPIC FORCEPS BIOPSY:
   -- MODERATELY DIFFERENTIATED (GRADE 2) INVASIVE ADENOCARCINOMA
   -- See comment.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:

Hematoxylin and eosin-stained sections show six fragments of colonic mucosa and submucosa, variably distorted by infiltrating neoplastic glands. The tumor is composed of irregular, angulated glands lined by stratified columnar epithelial cells with moderately pleomorphic, hyperchromatic nuclei and conspicuous nucleoli. Luminal "dirty" necrosis (comedonecrosis) is present within the neoplastic glands, a finding characteristic of colorectal adenocarcinoma. Desmoplastic stromal reaction surrounds the infiltrating glands. Foci of extracellular mucin production are noted, comprising less than 10% of the tumor cross-sectional area (not sufficient to qualify as mucinous adenocarcinoma). Tumor cells show loss of normal nuclear polarity. Mitotic figures are readily identified (approximately 4-5 per 10 HPF), with occasional atypical forms. No signet ring cell morphology is identified. No evidence of squamous differentiation. The background mucosa, where evaluable, shows reactive/regenerative changes but no unequivocal dysplastic polyp or serrated lesion architecture in the submitted fragments.

The overall morphology is consistent with a conventional colorectal adenocarcinoma, moderately differentiated (Grade 2), arising in the sigmoid colon.

IMPORTANT ANCILLARY STUDIES NOTE: Immunohistochemistry for mismatch repair (MMR) proteins has been performed and resulted (see below). Molecular/mutational analysis (KRAS, NRAS, BRAF) has been performed by the Molecular Diagnostics Laboratory on this specimen and resulted (see below). Additional tumor molecular profiling (comprehensive NGS panel) has been ordered and is PENDING at the time of this report; results to be issued as an addendum.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES:

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY FOR MISMATCH REPAIR (MMR) PROTEINS:
Performed on paraffin-embedded tissue sections from specimen A. Internal positive controls (normal colonic epithelium) confirmed adequate staining in all four assays.

  MLH1  (clone ES05; Leica Biosystems):    RETAINED nuclear expression
  MSH2  (clone FE11; Leica Biosystems):    RETAINED nuclear expression
  MSH6  (clone EP49; Leica Biosystems):    RETAINED nuclear expression
  PMS2  (clone EP51; Leica Biosystems):    RETAINED nuclear expression

Interpretation: Retained expression of all four mismatch repair proteins. This result is CONSISTENT WITH A MICROSATELLITE-STABLE (MSS) TUMOR. Loss of expression of one or more MMR proteins was NOT observed. This finding suggests low likelihood of Lynch syndrome-associated mismatch repair deficiency; however, correlation with clinical history and genetic counseling referral may be appropriate if clinical suspicion persists.

MOLECULAR DIAGNOSTICS -- HOTSPOT MUTATION ANALYSIS:
Performed on DNA extracted from formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) biopsy tissue (specimen A) by the Molecular Pathology Laboratory. Testing platform: PCR-based next-generation sequencing hotspot panel targeting KRAS exons 2-4 (codons 12, 13, 59, 61, 117, 146), NRAS exons 2-4, and BRAF codon 600.

  KRAS ANALYSIS (Exons 2-4):
    Codon 12: p.G12D (c.35G>A) MUTATION DETECTED
      Variant allele frequency (VAF): approximately 41%
      This is a well-characterized, oncogenic gain-of-function mutation in KRAS. KRAS G12D is one of the most common oncogenic alterations in colorectal adenocarcinoma, conferring constitutive RAS-MAPK pathway activation.
    Codons 13, 59, 61, 117, 146: No mutations detected

  NRAS ANALYSIS (Exons 2, 3, 4):
    Codons 12, 13, 59, 61, 117, 146: No mutations detected (NRAS wild-type)

  BRAF ANALYSIS:
    Codon 600 (V600E, V600K): No mutation detected (BRAF V600E wild-type)

Interpretation:
  - KRAS codon 12 mutation detected: p.G12D. This mutation is associated with resistance to anti-EGFR monoclonal antibody therapy (cetuximab, panitumumab) and should be incorporated into clinical decision-making.
  - NRAS: wild-type across all tested exons.
  - BRAF: V600E wild-type. Note: BRAF wild-type status in a KRAS-mutant MSS colorectal tumor is the expected and most common constellation. Extended BRAF testing (non-V600E mutations) is not part of this panel.

COMPREHENSIVE NGS TUMOR PROFILING: ORDERED -- PENDING. Results to be reported as addendum when available. (Estimated turnaround: 10-14 business days.)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A: Received in formalin, labeled with the patient's name and "sigmoid colon mass biopsy." The specimen consists of six fragments of tan-pink to tan-white soft tissue, irregular in configuration, ranging from 0.1 cm to 0.4 cm in greatest dimension. The tissue fragments are entirely submitted in one cassette (A1) following routine fixation. The aggregate tissue volume is approximately 0.4 x 0.4 x 0.2 cm when assembled. No grossly recognizable polypoid configuration is identified; fragments are friable and hemorrhagic, consistent with endoscopic biopsy material from a mass lesion.

Tissue processing: Routinely processed, paraffin-embedded. H&E staining performed. Unstained sections retained on charged slides for potential additional immunohistochemical or special stains.

  Cassettes submitted:
    A1: All tissue, entirely submitted (6 fragments)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Sections of the sigmoid colon biopsy show mucosal and submucosal fragments extensively replaced and infiltrated by irregularly-shaped neoplastic glands with a cribriform and fused architecture in areas. The neoplastic epithelium demonstrates nuclear enlargement, coarse chromatin, prominent nucleoli (1-2 per cell), and loss of nuclear polarity with pseudostratification. Apoptotic bodies are frequent. Intraluminal necrotic debris ("dirty necrosis") is a consistent finding across multiple gland profiles. A moderate desmoplastic stroma is present. Scattered lymphocytic infiltrate in the stroma is noted but intraepithelial lymphocytes are not conspicuously increased. No tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes at the density characteristic of MMR-deficient (MSI-high) colorectal cancers are identified, consistent with the MMR IHC results above. Lamina propria and submucosal tissue partially sampled show no vascular or perineural invasion identifiable in the biopsy material (evaluation limited in biopsy context). No adjacent adenomatous polyp is identified in the available material.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST ATTESTATION:

I have reviewed and interpreted all slides for this case and render the above diagnosis.

[ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST SIGNATURE BLOCK REDACTED]
Board-Certified Anatomic and Clinical Pathologist
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ELECTRONICALLY SIGNED AND FINALIZED
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

DISTRIBUTION: Ordering provider (Gastroenterology), Primary Care Physician, Medical Oncology (automatic routing per tumor board protocol for new colorectal malignancy diagnoses)

** ADDENDUM PENDING: Comprehensive NGS/tumor profiling panel results. Addendum to follow upon completion. **
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
